description:
Source: raw/platonism/plato-symposium.txt — 117,872 chars, single file (format=html, Jowett 1871).

Audit findings:
- Head: minor sacred-texts crumb ('Sacred Texts Classics Plato'), then clean Jowett title page.
- Tail: ends with '-THE END-'.
- No usable in-body section markers. Jowett renders dialogue as continuous narrative prose ('he said,' 'I said'); no Stephanus pagination; no headings between speeches.
- Speaker labels (SOCRATES, DIOTIMA, etc.) appear only once each, in the all-caps PERSONS OF THE DIALOGUE list at top.

Recommended strategy: paragraph-group (like the long Boehme excerpts).

pre_strip:
  - leading 'Sacred Texts Classics Plato' crumb
  - trailing '-THE END-'

Splitting by encomium speech (Phaedrus → Pausanias → Eryximachus → Aristophanes → Agathon → Socrates/Diotima → Alcibiades) would require manual offsets and is not worth doing in v1.
